GREAT WRONG DONE

pages.raw_text:
tne pIeSlucHt Ol tue CITY council.
Here is the statement of Clark:


GREAT WRONG DONE.


"In the case Of J. H. Elliott a
great wrong has been done an old
soldier and his family, without any
justification therefor" said Mr..
Clark in discussing the case. 1t is
beyond the power Of the relief COM
mittee Of Reno post to offer redress
for this wrong, but we demand that
justice be done in this case.


The only way that justice can be
had is through an appeal to the pub
lie conscience and the integrity and
honesty Of the loyal people of Spo-
kane.


Sufficient has come to light
through the emasculated investiga-
ton Of the partisan committee ap-
pointed by Mayor Pratt to show the
need of a complete and thorough in
vestigation of the facts in the case.
I am informed that sworn charges
have been filed against the police
department, which are to be heard
by the board of police commission
ers. AS this matter has twice been
before the heads Of the police de-
partment, without action either
time, hereby, in the name OF the
Grand Army Of the Republic, de-
mand that a fair and unbiased COM
mittee be now appointed by differ
ent civic bodies and the Grand
Army to go to the bottom Of this
case, and at the same time inquire
into all other charges that may be
made against the police.


81 would suggest that this COM
mittee consist of five members,
one to be appointed by the Won
ans club, one by the Grand Army.
one by the Central Labor council,
one by the Allied German societies
and the other by the president of
the city council. The civic organi-
cations named have been more or
less active in this matter and are
the proper bodies to take up this
investigation and see that right
prevails"
